text:
PET-CT SCAN WITH 18F-FDG (WHOLE BODY WITH DEDICATED NECK)

EXAMINATION: PET-CT, 18F-fluorodeoxyglucose, whole body with dedicated head and neck protocol
INDICATION: 54-year-old female with history of HPV-positive (p16+) squamous cell carcinoma, right base of tongue (T2N1M0 by conventional AJCC 8th edition staging; stage I by HPV-positive oropharyngeal staging criteria), treated with definitive concurrent cisplatin-based chemotherapy and intensity-modulated radiation therapy (IMRT), completed approximately 24 months prior. Imaging obtained for two-year post-treatment oncologic surveillance.
COMPARISON: Prior surveillance PET-CT performed approximately 9 months post-treatment; prior surveillance MRI neck performed approximately 18 months post-treatment; prior restaging PET-CT performed approximately 10 weeks post-treatment.

---

TECHNIQUE: Following intravenous injection of 10.2 mCi of 18F-FDG, whole-body PET imaging was performed from the vertex of the skull through the proximal thighs after an uptake period of 60 minutes. Blood glucose at the time of injection was 87 mg/dL. Low-dose CT images were acquired for attenuation correction and anatomic localization. Dedicated high-resolution CT images of the neck were obtained with IV contrast for anatomic characterization of the head and neck region.

---

FINDINGS:

HEAD AND NECK -- PRIMARY SITE:
The right base of tongue demonstrates no abnormal FDG activity. The maximum standardized uptake value (SUVmax) in this region measures 1.7, which is well within the range of normal background mucosal and lymphoid tissue activity for this anatomic site. No focal, asymmetric, or pathologically elevated metabolic activity is identified at the primary tumor bed. There is subtle mucosal thickening and mild T2 signal heterogeneity in the right base of tongue on the fused images, appearing stable to slightly improved compared to prior examinations and consistent with post-radiation fibrotic change. No discrete enhancing mass. The right vallecula, bilateral tonsillar fossae, soft palate, posterior pharyngeal wall, and hypopharynx are unremarkable without focal FDG uptake above background. The left base of tongue demonstrates symmetric physiologic activity (SUVmax 1.6). The larynx, including the epiglottis, aryepiglottic folds, and true and false vocal cords, are unremarkable without hypermetabolic activity.

REGIONAL LYMPH NODES:
The previously documented right level IIA lymph node, which had demonstrated avid FDG uptake at staging (SUVmax 9.8), is no longer metabolically active. A small residual node in this location measures approximately 0.6 cm in short axis and demonstrates an SUVmax of 1.4, entirely in keeping with a small benign or fibrotic lymph node. No internal necrosis. No pathologic FDG activity is identified within the right or left cervical nodal chains at any level (levels I-VI bilaterally). No retropharyngeal, parapharyngeal, or posterior cervical triangle nodal hypermetabolism. No supraclavicular nodal FDG uptake bilaterally. Overall, the bilateral cervical nodal stations are unremarkable.

SALIVARY GLANDS:
The parotid glands appear mildly reduced in volume bilaterally, somewhat more pronounced on the right, consistent with expected post-radiation atrophy and fatty replacement, a finding that has been progressive compared to pre-treatment examinations. No focal FDG-avid lesions within the parotid or submandibular glands. The sublingual glands are unremarkable. No suspicious nodules or calculi.

THYROID GLAND:
The thyroid gland demonstrates homogeneous, symmetric, low-level background uptake without focal FDG-avid nodules or masses (thyroid SUVmax 1.3). The gland is mildly reduced in volume compared to earlier imaging, a well-recognized post-radiation change following neck irradiation. No dominant nodule, calcification, or cystic change identifiable on the CT component. No thyroid hypermetabolism. Findings are stable in comparison to prior studies.

CHEST:
The lungs are clear throughout. There are no pulmonary nodules, masses, consolidation, or pleural effusions. Mediastinal and hilar lymph nodes are within normal size limits and show no significant FDG uptake. No mediastinal mass. The heart appears morphologically normal. There is no pericardial effusion. Thoracic aorta is unremarkable. No axillary adenopathy. The osseous structures of the thorax demonstrate no lytic or sclerotic lesions and no FDG-avid bone marrow abnormalities.

ABDOMEN AND PELVIS:
The liver is normal in size and homogeneous in density without focal lesions. No hepatic FDG uptake beyond background (liver SUVmax used as reference: 2.4). The spleen is within normal size limits without focal lesions or hypermetabolism. The pancreas, adrenal glands, and kidneys are unremarkable. No abdominal or retroperitoneal adenopathy. Normal physiologic FDG activity is seen in the bowel and genitourinary collecting systems, without discrete focal lesions. No pelvic masses. No pelvic or inguinal adenopathy of concern. The uterus and adnexa are unremarkable.

SKELETAL SURVEY:
No lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions. No FDG-avid bone lesions suggesting metastatic involvement. There is mild degenerative change in the lumbar spine and bilateral facet joints, without associated hypermetabolism. The cervical vertebrae and skull base demonstrate no worrisome osseous or FDG-avid findings. No pathologic fracture.

SOFT TISSUES:
No soft tissue masses or FDG-avid subcutaneous lesions. Mild post-radiation soft tissue changes in the right neck are noted on CT (mild subcutaneous thickening, reticular density in the right neck soft tissues), stable compared to prior examinations and consistent with expected post-radiation fibrosis. No lymphedema or significant soft tissue swelling.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. No FDG-avid disease at the right base of tongue primary tumor site. The SUVmax in this region (1.7) is at or below physiologic background, and no metabolically active lesion is identified. This is consistent with durable complete metabolic response at the primary site, now confirmed across multiple post-treatment imaging time points.

2. The previously involved right level IIA lymph node has remained metabolically quiescent, with a small residual fibrotic-appearing node of 0.6 cm and SUVmax 1.4. No locoregional nodal recurrence identified.

3. No evidence of distant metastatic disease in the chest, abdomen, pelvis, or skeleton.

4. No FDG-avid thyroid nodule. Thyroid volume mildly reduced, in keeping with post-radiation changes; clinical correlation with thyroid function tests is recommended as per standard survivorship care.

5. Post-radiation atrophic changes in the bilateral parotid glands, right greater than left, and soft tissue post-radiation fibrosis in the right neck -- all stable and consistent with expected late sequelae of prior neck irradiation.

Overall, this two-year post-treatment surveillance PET-CT demonstrates no radiographic or metabolic evidence of locoregional recurrence or distant metastatic spread. These findings are highly reassuring in the context of HPV-positive oropharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma treated with definitive chemoradiation.

Interpreting Radiologist: [Signature on file]
Dictated and finalized electronically.
